problems.stem: Under no circumstances employees are permitted to share their security credentials with anyone, regardless of their rank or position.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: employees are → are employees
problems.explanation: employees are → are employees

problems.stem: So profound the implications of the discovery were that the scientific community took nearly a decade to fully accept them.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: profound the implications... were → profound were the implications...
problems.explanation: profound the implications... were → profound were the implications...

problems.stem: Had the regulator acted more decisively, not only the financial crisis could have been averted but also public trust in the banking system might have been preserved.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: the financial crisis could → could the financial crisis
problems.explanation: the financial crisis could → could the financial crisis

problems.stem: The report fails to address the crucial question of how can the new policy be implemented without causing significant disruption to existing operations.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: how can the new policy be → how the new policy can be
problems.explanation: how can the new policy be → how the new policy can be

problems.stem: Rarely in the history of modern medicine a single invention has had such a transformative impact on public health as the development of vaccines.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: a single invention has → has a single invention
problems.explanation: a single invention has → has a single invention

problems.stem: On the wall of the ancient tomb ________ intricate hieroglyphs depicting the life and death of a forgotten pharaoh.
problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.explanation: (B) (주어는 intricate hieroglyphs)

problems.stem: Only by understanding the historical context of the novel one can fully appreciate the depth of its social commentary.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: one can → can one
problems.explanation: one can → can one

problems.stem: Such was the chaos after the earthquake that the government struggled to provide even basic necessities to its citizens.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: No Error

problems.stem: The committee is investigating why did the safety protocols fail and who is ultimately responsible for the oversight.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: why did the safety protocols fail → why the safety protocols failed
problems.explanation: why did the safety protocols fail → why the safety protocols failed

problems.stem: No sooner the CEO had finished his speech than the company's stock price began to plummet.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: the CEO had → had the CEO
problems.explanation: the CEO had → had the CEO

problems.stem: ________ aware of the potential risks, they would have never approved the project in the first place.
problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.explanation: (B) (가정법 과거완료 도치)

problems.stem: In the valley below the towering mountains a small, isolated village lies, seemingly untouched by the passage of time.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: a small... village lies → lies a small... village

problems.stem: Not for a single moment I did doubt his loyalty, even when all the evidence seemed to point against him.
problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 고치시오.
problems.answer: I did → did I
problems.explanation: I did → did I

problems.stem: The question of what constitutes "intelligence" and how can it be measured accurately remains a subject of intense debate among psychologists.

problems.answer: how can it be → how it can be

problems.stem: Little the board of directors knew that their decision to merge with the rival company would eventually lead to its downfall.

problems.answer: the board... knew → did the board... know

problems.stem: Into the crowded courtroom he walked, exuding an air of confidence that seemed to defy the gravity of the charges against him.

problems.stem: Not only the new regulations are difficult to interpret, but they also impose a significant financial burden on small businesses.

problems.answer: the new regulations are → are the new regulations

problems.stem: Should you require any further clarification on this matter, ________ hesitate to contact my office directly.

problems.stem: The police asked the witness to describe exactly what did he see on the night of the incident.

problems.stem: Hardly the sun had risen when the rescue team began their search for the missing hikers in the treacherous mountain terrain.

problems.stem: On no account you should provide personal information to anyone over the phone, even if they claim to be from your bank.

problems.stem: So skillfully the artist manipulated light and shadow that her paintings seemed almost three-dimensional.

problems.stem: Not until all other options had been exhausted the committee did agree to consider such a drastic measure.

problems.stem: Were the government to implement a universal basic income, the potential economic and social consequences ________ profound.
